tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 Day 1 at the All England Club Monday, June 29: the main draw opens across SW19 with defending champion Jannik Sinner starting Centre Court against Miomir Kecmanovic at 1:30pm BST alongside world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka and seven-time winner Novak Djokovic vs Yibing Wu; Emma Raducanu, Daniil Medvedev, Felix Auger-Aliassime, and Coco Gauff headline outer courts as first-round play begins in London • [SQUASH] PSA World Tour enters its summer hiatus after Leandro Romiglio's Madrid Squash Open triumph June 27 — the Italian No. 5 seed edged Marek Panacek 9-11, 11-7, 18-16, 11-9 in the final at Club de Squash Tres Cantos, capping a season that ended with Diego Elias and Hania El Hammamy lifting Tour Finals titles in Paris before the 2026-27 campaign resumes in August • [BADMINTON] YONEX US Open 2026 crowns new champions Sunday at Titan Gym in Fullerton: Su Li Yang defeats Kidambi Srikanth 15-21, 21-16, 9-21 in the men's singles final after ending teen qualifier Rounak Chouhan's dream run, while Denmark's Line Christophersen rallies past Bulgaria's Kaloyana Nalbantova 15-21, 21-11, 21-15 to lift the women's singles title at the $250K BWF Super 300 • [PICKLEBALL] PPA Tour eyes Singapore as the $70,000 Leapmotor Singapore Open — a PPA Asia 500 event — arrives in July at the Republic's highest-tier stop yet; Malaysia's Jimmy Liong says the city-state is ready for even bigger 1000-level events as Pickleball Inc.'s $225M Apollo-led raise fuels expansion across Asia, Australia, and Europe • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 Round of 64 heats up Monday at the Ontario Convention Center — Sora Matsushima sweeps Kirill Gerassimenko, Miwa Harimoto and Hina Hayata each win 3-0, and Wang Manyu cruises past American Erin Ye 11-5, 11-3, 11-4 as China's singles contingent posts six early main-draw wins on Day 2 of the $1.55M Grand Smash

newsItems.title: Su Li Yang and Christophersen Crown US Open Champions in Fullerton
newsItems.excerpt: The YONEX US Open 2026 closed Sunday, June 28, at Titan Gym in Fullerton, California, with new singles champions in both draws at the $250,000 BWF Super 300. Eighth seed Su Li Yang of Chinese Taipei denied former world No. 1 Kidambi Srikanth a long-awaited title, rallying for a 15-21, 21-16, 9-21 victory after Srikanth's grueling semifinal win over Yudai Okimoto. Su had ended Indian teen qualifier Rounak Chouhan's sensational run in the last four. In women's singles, Denmark's Line Christophersen claimed her first Super 300 title since the 2024 Hylo Open, coming from a set down to beat Bulgaria's Kaloyana Nalbantova 15-21, 21-11, 21-15 after semifinal wins over Devika Sihag and Rachel Chan respectively.

newsItems.title: PSA Tour Enters Summer Break After Madrid Challenger Wraps Season
newsItems.excerpt: Professional squash shifted into its off-season following the Madrid Squash Open 2026, which concluded Saturday, June 27, at Club de Squash Tres Cantos with Leandro Romiglio's 9-11, 11-7, 18-16, 11-9 final win over Czech qualifier Marek Panacek. Panacek had upset top seed Henry Leung en route to his first PSA final, but Romiglio held firm to close the last Challenger stop before the summer hiatus. The 2025-26 campaign had already ended at the PSA Squash Tour Finals in Paris, where Diego Elias became the first South American men's champion and Hania El Hammamy claimed her second women's title. The PSA World Tour resumes in August with the start of the 2026-27 season.

newsItems.title: Singapore Open Positions PPA Tour for Next Phase of Asian Growth
newsItems.excerpt: The PPA Tour is preparing for its highest-tier event in Singapore yet — the $70,000 Leapmotor Singapore Open, a PPA Asia 500 stop scheduled for July — as the sport's professional circuit scales across Southeast Asia. Malaysia's top-ranked player Jimmy Liong told The Straits Times the city-state is ready to host even larger PPA Asia 1000 events, citing strong local infrastructure and growing participation. Organizers describe the Singapore Open as part of a deliberate tiered pathway that includes 125-level feeders, 500 events, and elite 1000 and 1500 showcases in Kuala Lumpur, Hanoi, and Hong Kong. The push comes as Pickleball Inc. closed a $225 million Apollo Sports Capital investment in May, valuing the enterprise at $750 million and funding international expansion.

newsItems.title: Wang Manyu and Japanese Stars Advance at WTT United States Smash
newsItems.excerpt: Main-draw action accelerated Monday, June 29, at the WTT United States Smash 2026 in Ontario, California, as Round of 64 singles matches filled the Ontario Convention Center on Day 2 of the $1.55 million Grand Smash. Olympic champion Wang Manyu opened with a commanding 11-5, 11-3, 11-4 win over American Erin Ye and will next face Japan's Sato Hitomi, a former world championship bronze medalist. Fellow Japanese standouts Sora Matsushima, Miwa Harimoto, and Hina Hayata each posted straight-games victories, while China's singles contingent recorded six wins against three losses in early action. Defending men's champion Wang Chuqin leads a field that includes Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, Kanak Jha, and Lily Zhang through the July 5 finale.
